notes_from_pm: Lepsius, IV; Perring and Vyse, I of Giza; Reisner, G I; called Great or First Pyramid.
North Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, V; Perring and Vyse, 7 of Giza; Reisner, G I-a.
Middle Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, VI; Perring and Vyse, 8 of Giza; Reisner, G I-b.
South Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, VII; Perring and Vyse, 9 of Giza; Reisner, G I-c. Attributed to Henutsen (wife of Khufu).
Lepsius, VIII; Perring and Vyse, 2 of Giza; Reisner, G II; called Second Pyramid.
SUBSIDIARY PYRAMID WITH ‘SERDAB’. Reisner, G II-a.
Lepsius, IX; Perring and Vyse, 3 of Giza; Reisner, G III; called Third Pyramid.
East Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, XII; Perring and Vyse, 5 of Giza; Reisner, G III-a; sometimes called Fourth Pyramid.
Middle Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, XI; Perring and Vyse, 4 of Giza; Reisner, G III-b.
West Subsidiary Pyramid. Lepsius, X; Perring and Vyse, 6 of Gîza; Reisner, G III-c.
Judge and Strong-of-voice. Dyn. V.
Dyn. V.
Overseer of sculptors, King's waab-priest, Prophet of Sahureʿ and of the Pyramid of Sahureʿ. Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Userkaf, etc. Middle Dyn. V to Dyn. VI.
Late Dyn. IV or first half of Dyn. V.
Director of King's waab-priests, Inspector of tenants, etc. Dyn. V-VI.
Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserreʿ, Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu, etc. End of Dyn. V or Dyn. VI.
Inspector of craftsmen of the waabt, Overseer of the ten, etc. Dyn. VI.
Prophet of the Pyramids of Menkauhor and Neuserreʿ, Overseer of the new settlements, etc. Dyn. VI.
Late Dyn. V.
Overseer of the department of tenants of the Great House, Prophet of Khufu: Prophet of Reʿ in the Sun-temple of Neuserreʿ, etc. End of Dyn. V or later.
King's waab-priest. Dyn. V-VI.
King's son, Prophet of Heqet, heka-priest of Meḥyt, etc. Temp. Khufu.
Overseer of commissions, Director of bowmen. Probably temp. Khufu.
Middle Dyn. V or later.
Royal acquaintance. Temp. Khufu.
Inspector of waab-priests, etc. Middle Dyn. V or later.
Royal acquaintance (woman). Temp. Khufu or later.
Prophet of Khufu, Overseer of the Pyramid of Khufu, Overseer of the expedition, etc. Middle Dyn. V or later.
Royal acquaintance. Probably Dyn. V.
King's son, Overseer of the phylai of Upper Egypt, Greatest of the Ten of Upper Egypt. Temp. Khufu.
King's daughter. Middle Dyn. IV.
Royal acquaintance (woman). Middle or late Dyn. IV.
Not marked on plan.
Judge and Boundary official of the Great House, Overseer of tenants of the Great House, Noble of the King. Late Dyn. V or Dyn. VI.
Director of cattle-herdsmen and the pastures. Temp. Khufu.
King's waab-priest, Strong-of-voice of the granary of the interior (Xnw), Inspector of administrators, etc. Dyn. V.
Dyn. V-VI.
Tenant of the Great House, etc. Late Dyn. V or Dyn. VI.
Second half of Dyn. V. Architrave with figure of Khaʿkareʿ repeated nine times. Double-statue, Khaʿkareʿ, Hairdresser of the Great House.
Dyn. V-VI.
Dyn. V.
Prophet of Khufu, Inspector of waab-priests, etc. Middle Dyn. V or later.
Prophet of Khufu, Inspector of waab-priests, etc. Middle Dyn. V or later.
Prophet of Khufu, Overseer of the Pyramid-town of Khufu, Overseer of the waabt of the King, etc. Probably Dyn. V.
Position not marked on plan.
Mastaba north of Cemetery G 1200. Position not marked on plan.
Overseer of the House of weaving women, etc. Possibly late Dyn. IV.
Inspector of ka-servants. Possibly late Dyn. IV.
Mastaba north of Cemetery G 2000. Position not marked on plan.

TOMB OF HETEPHERES [I]. Temp. Khufu. Husband, Snefru. Son, Khufu. No superstructure. Re-burial, transferred from unidentified tomb probably at Dahshûr. Reisner Excavation. Harvard-Boston Expedition (1925-7).
Probably NEFERTKAU (woman). Temp. Khephren. Father, Snefru. Stone-built mastaba. No inscriptions found.
NEFERMAaET King's son, Hereditary prince, Overlord of Nekheb, etc. (Vizier of Khephren.) Temp. Khufu to Khephren. Mother, Nefertkau (tomb, probably, G 7050). Stone-built mastaba. LG 57.
SNEFRUKHAaEF Treasurer of the King of Lower Egypt, Herdsman of Apis, etc. Middle Dyn. IV to early Dyn. V. Father, Nefermaaet (tomb G 7060). Stone-built mastaba. LG 56.

MERYREaNUFER good name qAR Overseer of the Pyramid-towns of Khufu and Menkaurea, Inspector of waab-priests of the Pyramid of Khephren, Tenant of the Pyramid of Pepy I, etc. Temp. Pepy I or later. Wife, Gefi, Prophetess of Hathor, etc. Stone-built mastaba.

IDU Tenant of the Pyramid of Pepy I, Overseer of scribes of the meret, Inspector of wad-priests of the Pyramids of Khufu and Khephren, etc. Temp. Pepy I or later. Parents (possibly), Meryreanufer Kar and Gefi (tomb G 7101). Wife, Mertiotes. Stone-built mastaba.

G 7110+7120. KAWAaB King's eldest son of his body, Hereditary prince, Chief lector-priest, etc., and wife HETEPHERES [11] (later, wife of King Raazedef). Temp. Khufu. Father of Hetepheres [11], Khufu. Stone-built twin-mastaba. See also tomb G 7350.

G 7110+7120. KAWAaB King's eldest son of his body, Hereditary prince, Chief lector-priest, etc., and wife HETEPHERES [11] (later, wife of King Raazedef). Temp. Khufu. Parents of Kawaab, Khufu and Mertiotes [I] (eldest son). Stone-built twin-mastaba. See also tomb G 7350.
G 7130+7140. KHUFUKHAaEF [I] King's son of his body, Chief Justice and Vizier (probably of Khufu), etc., and wife NEFERTKAU. Temp. Khufu to end of Dyn. IV. Stone-built twin-mastaba.

G 7130+7140. KHUFUKHAaEF [I] King's son of his body, Chief Justice and Vizier (probably of Khufu), etc., and wife NEFERTKAU. Temp. Khufu to end of Dyn. IV. Parents of Khufukhaaef [I], Khufu and probably Henutsen. Stone-built twin-mastaba.
KHUFUKHAaEF [11] Greatest of the Ten of Upper Egypt, Belonging to the foremost seat, Secretary of his Lord. Temp. Neuserrea. Stone-built mastaba.
LG 8 I. Dyn. XXVI.
LG 83. aAHMOSI Commander of the army, and his mother Queen NEKHTUBASTERAU (wife of Amasis). Dyn. XXVI.
LG 84. PAKAP good name WEHEBREc-EMAKHET Overseer of scribes of the King's repast, etc. Dyn. XXVI. Entirely destroyed. 'Campbell's Tomb'.
LG 97. HARSIESI and HARWOZ both wnrw-priests. Dyn. XXVI. Mother, Tentamūn.
LG 100. SARCOPHAGUS-SHAPED TOMB OF KHENTKAUS [I] End of Dyn. IV or early Dyn. V. Mother of the two Kings of Upper and Lower Egypt, Daughter of the God, etc.
E. 'GREAT ENCLOSURE'. Probably Dyn. III
PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. ¹ [King's daughter of his] body, King's wife (of Teti), King's mother (of Pepy I).
PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF IPUT II. Dyn. VI.
H. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF ISESI. Dyn. V. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXVII; Perring and Vyse, 6; Haram el-Shawwaf.
PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF KHUIT. Dyn. VI. ¹ King's wife (of Teti).
I. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF MERENREʿ I. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXIX; Perring and Vyse, 8.
PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF NEIT. Dyn. VI.
C. STEP PYRAMID ENCLOSURE OF NETERIKHET (Zoser). Dyn. III. STEP PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXII; Perring and Vyse, 3; el-Haram el-Mudarrag.
G. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF PEPY I. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXVI; Perring and Vyse, 5.
K. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF PEPY II. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XLI; Perring and Vyse, 9.
D. STEP PYRAMID ENCLOSURE OF SEKHEMKHET. Dyn. III. STEP PYRAMID. Unfinished.
A. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF TETI. Dyn. VI. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXX; Perring and Vyse, 1.
PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXV; Perring and Vyse, 4.
B. PYRAMID-COMPLEX OF USERKAF. Dyn. V. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXXI; Perring and Vyse, 2; el-Haram el-Makharbish.
PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE OF WEZEBTEN. Dyn. VI
PYRAMID-ENCLOSURE PROBABLY OF WIFE OF ISESI. Dyn. V. PYRAMID. Lepsius, XXVIII; Perring and Vyse, 7.
